events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need reason. Scenario: commercial HVAC-R terminations & balancing, rough-complete. Field condition: damper positions vs. balance report mismatch. Reference SMACNA, TAB reports. Need estimate measurable condition and state why out of tolerance. We need decide pass/fail. Damper positions not matching TAB report means airflow balancing not achieved; out of tolerance. Decision fail. Risk? Could be medium/high. Commercial project, third-party inspections. Mismatch can cause inadequate ventilation, comfort, energy, code compliance. Risk high maybe. Component conditions: installed-defective. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 75-90? value? We can set value to mismatch count or percent deviation? Not asked numeric reading. Could set value to 0 or estimated percent mismatch. Since task says estimate or interpret measurable condition and state why out of tolerance. value maybe 0 default. But maybe set value to 100 (percent mismatch?) Hmm. The schema says value numeric reading or computed quantity when task asks for one. Not explicitly asked, but we can put 0. sound_source etc default empty string/0. workable empty list. order empty list. findings: damper positions do not match approved TAB balance report; airflow distribution out of balance. actions: verify damper positions against TAB report, adjust and lock dampers, re-balance and document, update as-built/TAB report. rationale: SMACNA and TAB require installed damper settings correspond to final balance report; mismatch indicates out-of-tolerance. references: SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction Standards, AABC/NEBB/ASHRAE 111 TAB standards. Need valid JSON only. Ensure no prose outside.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 80,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.85,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Installed damper positions do not match the approved TAB balance report.",
    "Airflow distribution is unbalanced and cannot be verified as code-compliant.",
    "Final balancing documentation does not reflect actual field conditions."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Verify every damper position against the approved TAB report.",
    "Adjust and lock dampers to the specified positions.",
    "Re-balance affected zones and update the TAB report accordingly.",
    "Submit corrected TAB documentation for inspector review before close-out."
  ],
  "rationale": "SMACNA and TAB standards require that installed damper settings correspond to the final approved balance report. A visible mismatch between field damper positions and the report means the system is out of tolerance, unbalanced, and not ready for inspection or acceptance.",
  "references": [
    "SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction Standards",
    "AABC / NEBB TAB standards",
    "ASHRAE 111 - Testing, Adjusting, and Balancing"
  ]
}
